title: silene flos-cuculi subsp. flos-cuculi
intro: Silene flos-cuculi subsp. flos-cuculi, also known as the red pimpernel or cowslip, is a perennial plant species within the carnation family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The plant has been associated with several health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating coughs, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis. In herbal preparations, Silene flos-cuculi subsp. flos-cuculi is often used as a tincture or infusate, and it can also be consumed as a tea to harness its medicinal properties.
